claim: Cross-check confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific configuration isolation with boundary mutation detection and cross-checker validation. All explorer evidence verified independently.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files with filesystem walk, and diffBoundarySnapshots compares SHA-256 digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms boundary detection algorithm works as described by explorer.

evidence.summary: Pre-execution baseline captured: const boundaryBefore = buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]);
evidence.why: Confirms clean baseline is established before any provider execution.

evidence.summary: Post-execution validation: boundaryAfter diff compared to boundaryBefore, creating BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any changes.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violation detection prevents silent project mutations.

evidence.summary: Three separate provider code paths: Codex (codex_sandbox, codex_approval, codex_model, outputFile), Claude (permission-mode, settings, tools, max_budget_usd, session persistence), Custom (custom_command via sh -c).
evidence.why: Independent read confirms provider-specific argument construction with no shared state.

evidence.summary: Claude provider normalizes agent_tool_profile to research/full/boundary, clears settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools, sets claude_no_session_persistence to true.
evidence.why: Confirms tool restriction flags are cleared and boundary relies on permission-mode and session persistence.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement groups results by claim, detects when recommendations include block vs proceed/clarify, creates EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blockers.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms cross-checker disagreement surfaces blockers instead of being averaged away.

evidence.summary: Test writes unexpected-project-write.txt during agent execution, expects blocked status and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary violation detection works in practice.

evidence.summary: Test creates git repo, agent writes untracked-mutation.txt, expects blocked status with boundary.changes detecting added file.
evidence.why: Test confirms git-backed boundary detection for untracked files.

evidence.summary: Test explorer recommends proceed, cross-checker recommends block with contradicting evidence, expects EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blocker and prd_ready false.
evidence.why: Test confirms cross-checker conflict prevents assumption promotion.

evidence.summary: Boundary blockers injected into readiness: boundaryBlockers mapped from boundaryChanges, then merged into readiness.blockers, forcing prd_ready false.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violations block PRD readiness even if agents pass.

risks: Real Claude -p agents may behave differently than test mocks, potentially exposing gaps in boundary detection or provider normalization.
Non-git repos with large file trees may experience slow boundary detection due to full filesystem walk.
Custom provider commands could theoretically bypass boundary if invoked without harness supervision.

result.notes: All 20 evidence points from explorer independently verified. No conflicts found. Provider isolation, boundary detection, and cross-checker validation are correctly implemented. Tests cover critical scenarios including provider isolation, boundary violations, and agent conflicts. System is ready for real Claude -p agent stress testing to validate production behavior.
